title: Create volumetric point cloud generator
summary: Implement a deterministic volumetric point cloud generator that produces 3D geometry with real spatial depth, integrating with the existing seeded PRNG and Three.js rendering pipeline.

steps.title: Create volumetric point generator module
steps.description: Implement generateVolumetricPoints(config) that returns a Float32Array of x,y,z positions. Include five shape generators: sphereVolume (cube-root sampling for uniform volume distribution), shell (spherical shell with thickness), torusVolume (torus with sqrt disk distribution in cross-section), noiseLattice (3D grid with sine-based displacement), and spiralField (spiral with radial/height modulation). All generators must use the seeded PRNG from src/visual/prng.ts for determinism.

constraints: All shapes must produce points with non-zero spread across X, Y, and Z axes
No shape may constrain points to a single plane (non-coplanar requirement)
Output must be deterministic for the same seed
Must handle edge cases: 0 points returns empty array, 1 point returns valid position
No NaN or Infinity values in output

steps.title: Write unit tests for volumetric generators
steps.description: Test all five shape generators for volumetric properties: non-zero spread on all three axes, non-coplanarity, determinism with same seed, variation with different seeds, correct output length, no NaN/Infinity values, and edge cases (0 and 1 point counts).

key_assertions: stdDev(x), stdDev(y), stdDev(z) all > threshold for each shape
Points are not coplanar (cross-product of point differences has non-zero magnitude)
Same seed + config produces identical Float32Array
No raw identifiers in output (privacy check)

steps.title: Integrate volumetric generator into point cloud system
steps.description: Update createPointCloud to use generateVolumetricPoints() for base geometry instead of any flat/2D distribution. Use the session seed PRNG to deterministically select a shape type. Apply optional lattice snapping when structureComplexity > 0.7 and angular variation when curveSoftness < 0.5. Wire density and maxPoints from quality tier into effective point count calculation.

integration_points: Shape selection: PRNG-driven from seed, weighted or uniform across five shapes
Post-generation transforms: lattice snapping, softness/angular variation
Geometry attributes: position, color, size, aHueOffset, aRandom
Must expose getPointCount() and getPointPositions() for testing

steps.title: Write integration tests for point cloud with volumetric geometry
steps.description: Add integration tests verifying the point cloud system produces volumetric 3D geometry: Z-depth spread, non-coplanarity after full pipeline, seed-driven shape variation across sessions, and determinism preservation through the integration layer.

key_assertions: Created mesh positions have non-zero Z spread (not flat plane)
Points remain non-coplanar after lattice snapping and other transforms
Different seeds produce different shape selections
Same seed produces identical geometry through full pipeline

steps.title: Verify build and acceptance criteria
steps.description: Run npm run build to confirm no compile errors. Run test suite to confirm all new and existing tests pass. Manually verify: base geometry occupies non-zero volume in X/Y/Z, geometry is not constrained to a single plane, point positions are generated in 3D space, and depth is visible through parallax when camera moves.
checks: Scene renders with and without autoplay

acceptance_criteria_mapping.Base geometry occupies non-zero volume i: Step 1 (all five generators produce volumetric output) + Step 2 (stdDev assertions on all axes)
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Geometry is not constrained to a single: Step 1 (non-coplanar by construction) + Step 2 (cross-product coplanarity test)
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Point positions are generated in 3D spac: Step 1 (Float32Array of x,y,z triples) + Step 3 (BufferGeometry position attribute)
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Depth is visible through parallax when c: Step 3 (volumetric positions in Three.js scene with perspective camera from US-039)

risks.risk: Performance with high point counts on low-end devices
risks.mitigation: Quality tier system already caps maxPoints per tier; density scaling reduces count on weaker hardware
Use seeded PRNG consistently; test determinism explicitly with byte-level Float32Array comparison

definition_of_done: No console errors on normal load
Scene works with and without successful autoplay
Mute toggle and info overlay still work
